task_completion.description: Open the Subsurface application and load the dive log
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Subsurface application window is open and displaying a list of dives.

task_completion.description: Select the dives from the December 2010 trip (Sund Rock)
task_completion.visual_evidence: Dives with dates starting with '2010-12' (December 2010) are selected or highlighted in the Subsurface dive list.

task_completion.description: Change the water type to 'Salt' for the target dives
task_completion.visual_evidence: The dive details or information panel shows the water type as 'Salt' (or salinity as 1030) for the selected December 2010 dives.

task_completion.description: Ensure dives outside of December 2010 are not modified
task_completion.visual_evidence: Dives from other dates, such as the '2011-09' trip, are visible and their water type remains 'Fresh Water' (or is clearly not changed to Salt).

task_completion.description: Save the updated dive log to ~/Documents/dives.ssrf
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog is visible saving the file to the 'Documents' folder as 'dives.ssrf', or a file manager/terminal shows 'dives.ssrf' newly created in the ~/Documents directory.

integrity.description: Agent used the Subsurface GUI to modify the dive logs
integrity.visual_evidence: The Subsurface application GUI is actively used to make the changes, rather than a text editor or terminal script.

integrity.description: Agent did not manually edit the raw XML file
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the agent opening the .ssrf file in a raw text editor (like nano, vim, or gedit) to manually find and replace text.

integrity.description: Agent used the application to save the file
integrity.visual_evidence: The file is saved using Subsurface's native 'Save' or 'Save As' functionality, rather than using terminal commands to copy or rename the original file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target dives to modify have the date prefix '2010-12' (Sund Rock). Control dives that must NOT be modified have the date prefix '2011-09' (Yellow House). In Subsurface, setting the water type to 'Salt' corresponds to an internal salinity value of 1030 g/l.
